member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.DomainEventBus
member.summary: A thread-safe, generic event bus responsible for handling domain events within a specific game domain (e.g., Combat, Inventory).

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.DomainEventBus.Subscribe``1(System.Action{``0})
member.summary: Subscribes a handler action to a specific type of domain event.
member.typeparam: The type of the event that this bus handles.
member.param: The action delegate to be called when an event of type TEvent is published.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.DomainEventBus.Unsubscribe``1(System.Action{``0})
member.summary: Unsubscribes a specific handler action from a given domain event type.
member.param: The specific action delegate to remove.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.DomainEventBus.Publish``1(``0)
member.summary: Publishes an event, notifying all subscribed handlers for that event type.
member.typeparam: The type of the event being published.
member.param: The concrete instance of the domain event.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.DomainEventBus.Clear
member.summary: Clears all subscriptions from the bus. Used for testing or scene reloads.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.GameServices
member.summary: Aggregates all domain event buses into a single service locator pattern, implementing the IGameServices interface. This class provides access points for various core game systems' event buses.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.GameServices.Clear
member.summary: Clears all underlying event buses. Should be called between scenes or during testing to prevent stale events from affecting subsequent game states.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.IntentBatcher
member.summary: Collects Intent events published during a scheduler phase and delivers them as a batch at the start of the next phase. This implements the two-step Intent→Granted/Refused pattern.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.IntentBatcher.Enqueue``1(``0)
member.summary: Enqueues an intent for batch delivery in the next phase.
member.typeparam: The type of the event (must inherit from IEvent).
member.param: The event instance to enqueue.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.IntentBatcher.Flush``1
member.summary: Dequeues all pending intents of a given type. Returns snapshot list and clears the queue for that type.
member.typeparam: The expected type of the event.
member.returns: A list containing copies of all pending events of type {TEvent}. Returns an empty list if no intents are pending.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.IntentBatcher.FlushAll
member.summary: Flushes all pending intents of all types. Returns dictionary mapping event Type to list of events. Clears all queues.
member.returns: A dictionary containing snapshots of all pending intents, keyed by their type.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.IntentBatcher.PendingCount``1
member.summary: Returns count of pending intents for a given type without flushing them.
member.typeparam: The expected type of the event.
member.returns: The number of pending events of type {TEvent}. Returns 0 if no intents are pending.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Bus.IntentBatcher.Clear
member.summary: Clears all pending intents without delivering them or generating any output.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.IComponentStore
T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1
member.summary: Типизированный storage для компонентов одного типа. Реализован с использованием паттерна SparseSet: плотный массив значений + разреженный массив индексов по EntityId. Это даёт O(1) Add/Remove/Get и cache-friendly итерацию по всем компонентам типа .

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.Count
member.summary: The number of components currently stored.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the ComponentStore{T} class.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.EnsureCapacity(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Ensures the underlying sparse array has enough capacity for the given EntityId index. Doubles capacity if required.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.Add(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId,`0)
member.summary: Adds a component for the specified entity. Overwrites if an existing component is present.
member.param: The unique identifier of the entity.
The component data to store.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.Remove(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Removes a component from the specified entity. If present, the slot is filled by swapping with the last stored component to maintain array density (O(1) removal).
member.param: The unique identifier of the entity.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.Has(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Determines whether or not the specified entity has a component of this type.
member.param: The unique identifier of the entity.
member.returns: True if the component is present; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.Get(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Gets the component for the specified entity. Throws InvalidOperationException if the component is absent.
member.param: The unique identifier of the entity.
member.returns: The stored component instance.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.TryGet(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId,`0@)
member.summary: Attempts to get the component for the specified entity. The operation is safe and returns false if absent.
member.param: The unique identifier of the entity.
When this method returns, contains the stored component instance, or default(T) if not found.
member.returns: True if the component was successfully retrieved; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.All
member.summary: Returns a read-only span over all component instances currently stored in the dense array. This provides an efficient way to iterate over all components without allocation overhead (hot path).
member.returns: ReadOnlySpan{T} containing all active components.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.ComponentStore`1.EnumerateIndices
member.returns: Sequence of entity indices for all live slots in this store.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.IModFaultSink
member.summary: Sink for mod-system faults reported from the isolation guard. Implemented in the Application layer by {ModFaultHandler} (Phase 2 completion is scheduled later — for now a no-op sink is enough). Core never crashes a mod's host process directly; it hands the fault off through this sink and throws {IsolationViolationException} with a mod-specific message, and the scheduler's caller decides what to do.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.IModFaultSink.ReportFault(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Reports a fault originating from the mod with the given identifier. Implementations typically log the fault and schedule the mod for unload.
member.param: Unique identifier of the offending mod.
Diagnostic message with the violation details.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.NullModFaultSink
member.summary: Default no-op {IModFaultSink} used when no Application-layer handler is wired in — keeps {SystemExecutionContext} functional in tests and Core-only scenarios. Production code in Application should replace this with the real {ModFaultHandler}.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.NullModFaultSink.ReportFault(System.String,System.String)
member.param: Ignored.
Ignored.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.IsolationViolationException
member.summary: Исключение, бросаемое SystemExecutionContext при нарушении изоляции: обращение к незадекларированному компоненту, прямой доступ к другой системе, использование World в обход сторожа. Сообщение содержит имя нарушающей системы, имя нарушенного типа и подсказку, как исправить декларацию.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.IsolationViolationException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Создаёт исключение с диагностическим сообщением. Формат сообщения фиксирован и используется тестами — не менять без согласования.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase
member.summary: Base class for all game systems in DualFrontier. Systems contain game logic and run every tick via ParallelSystemScheduler.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.Context
member.summary: Gets the execution context, which provides system access to component data and event buses. This property is set by ParallelSystemScheduler after construction. Internal setter — only scheduler assigns this.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.OnInitialize
member.summary: Called once when system is registered. Use for bus subscriptions or initial setup logic. Default implementation is empty and can be overridden by derived classes when needed.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.Update(System.Single)
member.summary: The main game logic update loop, called by the scheduler every tick according to [TickRate]. All system-specific game logic must be implemented here.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.OnDispose
member.summary: Called once when system is unregistered or game shuts down. Use this for cleanup, unsubscriptions, and resource release. Default implementation is empty and can be overridden by derived classes when needed.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.Initialize
member.summary: Internal method called by the scheduler to initialize the system's lifecycle hooks.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.Dispose
member.summary: Internal method called by the scheduler to dispose of the system's resources and logic.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.GetComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Reads a component of type {T} from entity via the thread-local execution context. Delegates to , which enforces the isolation guard in DEBUG. Throws {InvalidOperationException} when called outside a scheduler-pushed context (e.g. from the Godot main thread).
member.param: Target entity.
member.returns: The stored component instance.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.SetComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId,``0)
member.summary: Writes a component of type {T} on entity via the thread-local execution context. Delegates to , which enforces the isolation guard in DEBUG. Throws {InvalidOperationException} when called outside a scheduler-pushed context.
member.param: Target entity.
New component value.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.Query``1
member.summary: Enumerates entities that currently have a component of type {T}. Delegates to — lazy, DEBUG-guarded. Throws {InvalidOperationException} when called outside an active scheduler context.
member.typeparam: Component type whose owners to enumerate.
member.returns: Lazy sequence of entity ids carrying a component of type {T}.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.Query``2
member.summary: Enumerates entities that currently have both {T1} and {T2} components. Delegates to . Throws {InvalidOperationException} when called outside an active scheduler context.
member.returns: Lazy sequence of entity ids carrying both components.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.GetSystem``1
member.summary: Always throws via the thread-local execution context. Retained so that attempts to reach another system produce a clear, diagnosable error instead of a silent compile. Use {EventBus} for cross-system communication.
member.typeparam: System type the caller tried to resolve.
member.returns: Never returns — always throws.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase.Services
member.summary: Domain-bus aggregator supplied by the scheduler. Use for publishing events (Services.Pawns.Publish(...)) and subscribing in . Reads are routed through the active so that out-of-context calls (e.g. from the Godot main thread) fail loudly, and tests that build a context without supplying services receive a diagnostic instead of a silent NullReferenceException.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext
member.summary:
Isolation guard for systems. When a specific system is executing, holds its context: allowed READ/WRITE types, system name (for error messages), reference to {World}, and {SystemOrigin} — whether the system is Core or Mod.
Context is stored in {ThreadLocal{T}} — each scheduler thread has its own. The scheduler pushes the context before calling SystemBase.Update and pops it after (in a finally block).
Violation reaction depends on : Core systems throw (crash — this is a developer bug); Mod systems additionally route the diagnostic through {IModFaultSink}, so the Application layer's {ModFaultHandler} can unload the mod gracefully while the game continues running.
In Release builds, the access checks are elided by #if DEBUG, making / a thin pass-through with zero guard overhead. always throws regardless of build flavor — direct system-to-system references are a semantic architectural violation.
WARNING: context breaks with async/await inside a system — the continuation may land on a different thread where is null. async/await is strictly forbidden in Domain. See TechArch 11.7.

Creates a guard for the given system. The scheduler (or a test) populates allowed reads/writes/buses from the system's [SystemAccess] declaration. {Origin} decides whether violations crash ({Core}) or route through {IModFaultSink} ({Mod}). {ModId} is only meaningful when is . {Services} is optional — when null, systems that reach for SystemBase.Services receive an explicit error instead of silently publishing into a no-op bus.
member.param: Target world the guarded system may access.
Display name used in violation messages.
Component types the system may read.
Component types the system may write.
Bus names the system may publish to.
Core vs Mod provenance of the system.
Mod identifier when is Mod; otherwise null.
Destination for mod-origin fault reports.
Domain-bus aggregator exposed to the system via SystemBase.Services; null for tests that do not exercise publication.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.Services
member.summary: Domain-bus aggregator supplied by the scheduler. Null when no services were provided at construction time (isolated unit tests). Exposed internally; systems reach it via SystemBase.Services.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.Current
member.summary: Current execution context for the calling thread. Null when the calling thread does not belong to the scheduler (e.g. Godot main thread, a test that has not pushed a context).

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.PushContext(DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext)
member.summary: Pushes onto the calling thread's slot. Throws if a context is already set — indicates a scheduler bug (nested push without pop). Called by {ParallelSystemScheduler} before SystemBase.Update.
member.param: The context to make current on this thread.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.PopContext
member.summary: Pops the context from the calling thread's slot. Called by {ParallelSystemScheduler} in a finally block after SystemBase.Update so the slot is always cleared, even on exceptions.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.GetComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Reads the component of type {T} from entity . In DEBUG: throws if {T} is not declared in the system's [SystemAccess] reads or writes. In RELEASE: the check is elided — thin pass-through to World.GetComponentUnsafe.
member.param: Target entity.
member.returns: The stored component instance.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.SetComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId,``0)
member.summary: Writes a component of type {T} on entity . In DEBUG: throws if {T} is not declared in the system's [SystemAccess] writes. In RELEASE: the check is elided — thin pass-through to World.SetComponent.
member.param: Target entity.
New component value.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.Query``1
member.summary: Enumerates entities that currently have a component of type {T}. Lazy — uses yield return so iteration stops at the caller's break without materialising. In DEBUG: throws if {T} is not declared in the system's [SystemAccess] reads or writes.
member.typeparam: Component type whose owners to enumerate.
member.returns: Lazy sequence of entity ids carrying a component of type {T}.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.Query``2
member.summary: Enumerates entities that currently have both a {T1} and a {T2} component. Picks the smaller store to iterate and filters by HasComponent on the larger one. Lazy — no materialisation. In DEBUG: each type parameter is checked against declared reads or writes.
member.returns: Lazy sequence of entity ids carrying both components.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemExecutionContext.GetSystem``1
member.summary: Always throws . Direct system-to-system references break the architecture's isolation rules — use {EventBus} instead. This method exists intentionally so that attempting to reach a sibling system produces a clear, actionable diagnostic rather than a silent compile error.
member.typeparam: System type the caller tried to resolve.
member.returns: Never returns — always throws.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemOrigin
member.summary: Origin of a registered system. Determines reaction to isolation violations: Core systems crash with {IsolationViolationException}, mod systems route through {IModFaultSink} so the upper layer can unload the mod without taking the game down.

member.name: F:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemOrigin.Core
member.summary: System from DualFrontier.Systems or any first-party assembly. A violation here is a developer bug — crash loudly.

member.name: F:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemOrigin.Mod
member.summary: System loaded from a user mod through ModLoader. A violation here unloads the mod and keeps the game running.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World
member.summary:
Registry of all entities and their components. One store per component type; queries return intersections of those stores.
INVARIANT — deferred destruction: DestroyEntity must NOT remove components from their stores immediately. Removal happens at the END of the current scheduler phase, after all systems in that phase have finished iterating. This makes lazy Query iterators safe: a system that holds a reference to an entity mid-iteration will not see a "swap-with-last" hole in the SparseSet dense array.
Consequence: between DestroyEntity and end-of-phase cleanup, IsAlive returns false (version incremented immediately), but GetComponentUnsafe may still return stale data for that slot. Systems MUST check IsAlive before acting on a component.
WARNING: if you ever relax the no-async rule in Domain, this invariant breaks — await points can suspend a system mid-iteration and let another system run, defeating the phase-boundary protection. Do not introduce async/Task in Domain code.
ACCESS RULE: Systems must never call World methods directly. All access goes through , which enforces the [SystemAccess] declaration and throws on violations.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.CreateEntity
member.summary: Creates a new entity. Recycles freed slots when available; otherwise allocates the next slot, growing the version array if needed.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.IsAlive(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Returns true if the entity slot version matches — entity is alive. Safe to call from any thread (versions written only by , serialised through the scheduler).

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.DestroyEntity(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Marks entity for destruction. Increments slot version immediately (so returns false at once), then queues the entity for component removal at end-of-phase via .

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.FlushDestroyedEntities
member.summary: Flushes pending destruction: removes all components from stores and recycles slot indices. Called by ParallelSystemScheduler at end-of-phase only — never mid-phase.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.AddComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId,``0)
member.summary: Adds or overwrites a component for the given entity.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.RemoveComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Removes a component from the given entity. No-op if absent.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.HasComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Returns true if the entity has a component of type {T}.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.TryGetComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId,``0@)
member.summary: Tries to get a component. Returns false if absent.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.GetComponentUnsafe``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: UNSAFE direct component access — only for . Bypasses isolation checks. Do not call from game logic.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.SetComponent``1(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId,``0)
member.summary: UNSAFE direct write — only for . Alias for used by the isolation guard for write-side semantics.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.GetEntitiesWith``1
member.summary: Enumerates the live entities that currently carry a component of type {T}. Used by to implement Query<T>. Iterates the matching and reconstructs each {EntityId} from the slot's current version. Returns an empty sequence if no store of type {T} has ever been created.
member.returns: Lazy sequence of entity ids with a component of type {T}.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World.GetComponentCount``1
member.summary: Returns the number of components of type {T} currently stored. Used by to pick the smaller store to iterate. Returns 0 if no store of type {T} exists.
member.returns: Live count of components of type {T} in this world.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.ECS.IRemovable
member.summary: Internal interface allowing to call on a non-generic reference.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid
member.summary: Implements a spatial partitioning grid for efficient neighbour queries and collision detection.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with specified dimensions.
member.param: The width (number of columns) of the grid.
The height (number of rows) of the grid.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.CalculateIndex(DualFrontier.Contracts.Math.GridVector,System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the flat index for a given GridVector position, assuming row-major order.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.Add(DualFrontier.Contracts.Math.GridVector,DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Adds an entity to the specified tile if it is within bounds. This operation has O(1) complexity.
member.param: The world coordinates (tile position) of the entity.
The unique identifier of the entity.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.Remove(DualFrontier.Contracts.Math.GridVector,DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Removes an entity from the specified tile if it is present and within bounds. This operation has O(1) complexity.
member.param: The world coordinates (tile position) of the entity.
The unique identifier of the entity.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.Move(DualFrontier.Contracts.Math.GridVector,DualFrontier.Contracts.Math.GridVector,DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Moves an entity from one tile to another atomically by removing it from the old tile and adding it to the new tile. This operation has O(1) complexity (assuming both tiles are in bounds).
member.param: The starting world coordinates of the entity.
The destination world coordinates of the entity.
The unique identifier of the entity being moved.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.GetAt(DualFrontier.Contracts.Math.GridVector)
member.summary: Gets all entities currently present at a specific tile position. Returns an empty collection if the position is out of bounds or contains no entities. The returned collection represents a snapshot, and modification to it will not affect the grid state.
member.param: The world coordinates (tile position) to query.
member.returns: A read-only collection of entity IDs at that tile.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.GetInRadius(DualFrontier.Contracts.Math.GridVector,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets all entities within a square radius (Chebyshev distance) from a central point. This query iterates over the bounding box defined by the radius and is intended for non-hot-path use. Allocates a new list of entity IDs.
member.param: The center tile position for the search.
The maximum Chebyshev distance from the center.
member.returns: A list containing all unique entity IDs found in the specified radius.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.RemoveAll(DualFrontier.Contracts.Core.EntityId)
member.summary: Removes an entity from all tiles throughout the entire grid. This operation is O(Width*Height).
member.param: The unique identifier of the entity to remove globally.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.Clear
member.summary: Clears all entities from every tile in the spatial grid, resetting the grid to an empty state.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.Width
member.summary: Gets the total width of the grid.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.Math.SpatialGrid.Height
member.summary: Gets the total height of the grid.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.DependencyGraph
member.summary:
System-dependency graph built from [SystemAccess] declarations. An edge A → B means "B depends on A" — B must run after A because B reads a component that A writes. performs Kahn's topological sort and groups mutually independent systems into instances consumable by ParallelSystemScheduler.
Write-write collisions on the same component type are reported as errors at time. Cycles are detected after the topological sort drains and are likewise reported as errors. Diagnostic messages follow the format documented in docs/THREADING.md.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.AddSystem(DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase)
member.summary: Registers a system with the graph. Verifies the concrete type carries and is not already registered. Must be called before .

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.Build
member.summary: Builds the graph: reads every [SystemAccess] declaration, detects write-write conflicts, creates write-to-read edges, and runs Kahn's topological sort into ordered phases. Throws with a formatted diagnostic on write conflicts or cycles. Must be called exactly once after all systems have been registered via .

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.GetPhases
member.summary: Returns the phases produced by , in execution order. Throws if has not yet been called.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.DependencyGraph.Reset
member.summary: Clears all registered systems, edges, and phases. Allows the graph to be repopulated and rebuilt. Used by tests and future mod hot-reload.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.ParallelSystemScheduler
member.summary:
Parallel system scheduler. Consumes the ordered phase list produced by and executes them sequentially: within a phase, systems run concurrently via Parallel.ForEach; between phases, the blocking semantics of Parallel.ForEach provide an implicit barrier.
Per-system lifecycle inside a phase: 1. filters out systems not due on this tick according to their [TickRate]. 2. SystemExecutionContext.PushContext establishes the isolation guard on the current thread. 3. SystemBase.Update runs. 4. SystemExecutionContext.PopContext clears the guard — always, even if Update throws.
Exceptions from SystemBase.Update are not caught here — they propagate through Parallel.ForEach as an AggregateException. This is deliberate: during development we want system faults to surface immediately. Future phases introduce ModFaultHandler, which wraps mod-origin systems; Core systems continue to propagate.
The MaxDegreeOfParallelism follows the documented N-2 rule — reserving one core for Godot's main thread and one for the OS and background work. See docs/THREADING.md.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.ParallelSystemScheduler.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.SystemPhase},DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickScheduler,DualFrontier.Core.ECS.World,DualFrontier.Core.ECS.IModFaultSink,DualFrontier.Contracts.Bus.IGameServices)
member.summary: Creates a scheduler bound to the given phase list, tick clock, and target world. The MaxDegreeOfParallelism is fixed at construction time to max(1, ProcessorCount - 2). Execution contexts are pre-built for every registered system so the per-tick hot path does no reflection or allocation.
member.param: Phases in execution order as produced by .
Tick clock used to filter systems by [TickRate].
Target world the systems act upon.
Optional sink for mod-origin faults; defaults to a no-op sink.
Optional domain-bus aggregator surfaced to systems via SystemBase.Services; null for tests that never publish.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.ParallelSystemScheduler.ExecutePhase(DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.SystemPhase,System.Single)
member.summary: Executes a single phase: every system whose [TickRate] is due on the current tick runs on a pool thread. Parallel.ForEach blocks until all systems in the phase have completed, which forms the phase barrier.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.ParallelSystemScheduler.ExecuteTick(System.Single)
member.summary: Executes one full game tick: every phase in order with the current tick value, then advances the tick counter. The first call executes on tick 0; subsequent calls execute on tick 1, 2, and so on.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.ParallelSystemScheduler.Rebuild(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.SystemPhase})
member.summary: Replaces the active phase list with a new one produced after mod integration. Must be called only from the menu (not during a game session). The per-system execution-context cache is rebuilt for the new system set using the same construction rules as the constructor.
member.param: Phases in execution order as produced by .

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.ParallelSystemScheduler.Phases
member.summary: Returns the currently installed phase list. Exposed for tests that observe the atomic-rebuild contract: on failure the old list must remain referentially identical.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.SystemPhase
member.summary: Набор систем, которые могут исполняться параллельно (не имеют общих WRITE). Неизменяемая структура: строится один раз и далее только читается планировщиком.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.SystemPhase.Systems
member.summary: Системы этой фазы. Порядок произвольный — исполнение параллельное.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.SystemPhase.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase})
member.summary: Создаёт фазу из готового списка систем.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickRates
member.summary: Канонические значения частот тиков. Дублирует DualFrontier.Contracts.Attributes.TickRates — значения обязаны совпадать, иначе планировщик и атрибуты разойдутся. Дубль существует, чтобы системам Core не требовалось импортировать namespace атрибутов для рантайм-сравнений.

member.name: F:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickRates.REALTIME
member.summary: Каждый тик — физика снарядов, UI-реактивность.

member.name: F:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickRates.FAST
member.summary: Раз в 3 тика — бой, отзывчивые системы.

member.name: F:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickRates.NORMAL
member.summary: Раз в 15 тиков — обычная логика: работы, навыки, мана.

member.name: F:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickRates.SLOW
member.summary: Раз в 60 тиков (~1 раз/сек) — нужды, настроение, рост эфира.

member.name: F:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickRates.RARE
member.summary: Раз в 3600 тиков (~1 раз/мин) — социалка, рейды, торговля.

member.name: T:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickScheduler
member.summary:
Tracks the monotonically increasing game tick counter and decides whether a given system is due to run on the current tick according to its [TickRate] attribute. Consumed by ParallelSystemScheduler to filter systems before each phase.
The reflection lookup of is memoised per concrete system type so the hot path of does no reflection or allocation after the first call for each type.
Not thread-safe: accessed only from the scheduler's driver thread before parallel dispatch of a phase.

member.name: P:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickScheduler.CurrentTick
member.summary: The current tick number. Starts at 0 and is incremented by once per full ExecuteTick call.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickScheduler.Advance
member.summary: Increments the tick counter by 1. Called by ParallelSystemScheduler.ExecuteTick after every phase in the current tick has completed, so queries during the tick always see the same value.

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickScheduler.ShouldRun(DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase)
member.summary: Returns true if should run on the current tick according to its [TickRate]. Systems without the attribute default to (every tick).

member.name: M:DualFrontier.Core.Scheduling.TickScheduler.Reset
member.summary: Resets the tick counter to 0 and clears the tick-rate cache. Intended for test isolation and future mod hot-reload.

member.name: T:ComponentRegistry
member.summary: A registry that tracks which component types are known to the ECS. Used at startup to validate [SystemAccess] declarations.

member.name: M:ComponentRegistry.Register``1
member.summary: Registers a component type. No-op if already registered.
member.typeparam: The type of the component to register, must implement IComponent.

member.name: M:ComponentRegistry.IsRegistered``1
member.summary: Returns true if the type has been registered.

member.name: M:ComponentRegistry.IsRegistered(System.Type)
member.summary: Returns true if the type has been registered (non-generic overload).
member.param: The component type to check.

member.name: P:ComponentRegistry.All
member.summary: Returns all registered component types (snapshot).

member.name: M:ComponentRegistry.Clear
member.summary: Clears all registrations (used in tests).

member.name: T:SystemRegistry
member.summary: A registry that tracks all SystemBase instances registered for the game loop. Used by ParallelSystemScheduler to build the dependency graph.

member.name: M:SystemRegistry.Register(DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase)
member.summary: Registers a system. Throws ArgumentException if already registered.
member.param: The system to register.

member.name: M:SystemRegistry.Unregister(DualFrontier.Core.ECS.SystemBase)
member.summary: Unregisters a system. No-op if not found.
member.param: The system to unregister.

member.name: M:SystemRegistry.GetAll
member.summary: Returns all registered systems (snapshot as array).

member.name: P:SystemRegistry.Count
member.summary: Gets count of registered systems.

member.name: M:SystemRegistry.Clear
member.summary: Clears all registrations (used in tests).
